question: How should geometry_context_digest semantics be handled across mapping and detector stacks?

options.name: HARMONIZE_TO_MAPPING
options.definition: Use mapping digest payload (schema/sweep/cell/axis_levels/weighting_mode) as canonical digest everywhere, including detector outputs.

notes: Detector output digest values change for contexts with extra keys.
Historical detector artifacts become non-comparable unless migration mapping is retained.
Current divergence check would pass after harmonization.
Artifact graph simplification.
Could hide detector-context differences that are not in mapping identity payload.
May under-specify gate lineage if detector receives richer context.
Update detector to use mapping-style normalization before hashing.
Document canonical payload schema and missing-key behavior.

options.name: HARMONIZE_TO_DETECTOR
options.definition: Use full geometry_context object digest as canonical digest everywhere, including mapping artifacts.

notes: All mapping artifacts would change digest behavior immediately.
Any additional key in geometry_context changes identity unexpectedly.
Current divergence check would pass after harmonization.
But digest stability across schema evolution may degrade.
Experiment identity drift likely as non-core context fields are added.
Cross-run comparability may break when benign metadata changes.
Update mapping digest constructor and all downstream expectations.
Potentially version-lock geometry_context shape.

options.name: SPLIT_INTO_DISTINCT_DIGEST_TYPES
options.definition: Keep both digest scopes with explicit names and contracts (e.g., geometry_mapping_identity_digest and geometry_context_input_digest).

notes: Existing geometry_context_digest can be retained temporarily as alias in one stack.
Additive field introduction allows phased migration.
Rehearsal checks can validate both digests for intended semantics.
Divergence becomes expected/typed, not failure by default.
Supports deterministic cell identity and richer detector lineage simultaneously.
Allows explicit governance rules per digest type.
Requires schema updates across emitted artifacts and docs.
Needs migration window and naming governance, but no forced semantic loss.

comparative_summary.key_tradeoff: single canonical digest is simpler but collapses distinct semantics
